description: Cross-voting significantly impacted Rajya Sabha elections in Jharkhandand Legislative Council elections in Karnataka, with both the INDIA bloc and NDA facing losses. While parties lauded rival cross-voters as exercising conscience, they condemned their own MLAs for betraying the party. This highlights a persistent double standard, where power dynamics, not ideology, drive these electoral outcomes despite past reforms aimed at curbing corruption.

title: 21-year-old NEET aspirant dies after falling from 3rd floor in Indore
description: A 21-year-old medical aspirant in Indore, Avantika Maurya, tragically died after falling from a multi-storey building, two days before the NEET-UG re-test. Police are investigating the incident, which occurred while she was on a phone call. Her father mentioned she had been suffering from depression for five months. The incident has sparked political reactions, with the Congress blaming the ruling BJP over NEET irregularities.

title: India XI for Chennai ODI: Jaiswal set to open with Rohit, Nitish Reddy returns

Between Ishan Kishan, Shreyas Iyer and KL Rahul one will miss out as management wants all-rounders Washington Sundar and Nitish Kumar Reddy to get a fair run.

The actress, already mother to sons Jonathan and Jack, shared her delight in this phase of family life, describing it as a "wonderful time." Hathaway previously spoke about how motherhood profoundly transformed her perspective, making her feel more grounded and true to herself as she embraces this new chapter.

description: Uddhav Thackeray projected confidence amid a fresh party rebellion, assuring Shiv Sainiks are "fired up" despite six Lok Sabha MPs defecting. He apologized to voters for electing lawmakers who switched sides. Thackeray dismissed merger talks with Congress and defended his leadership, highlighting electoral wins. He warned of a threat to democracy with a move towards a "one party, no election" system. This latest crisis follows the 2022 split.

title: 'Just a trailer': Eknath Shinde warns as Uddhav Sena faces revolt 2.0
description: Shiv Sena (UBT)'s Lok Sabha unit appeared on the verge of a formal split on Thursday after six of its nine MPs stayed away from a parliamentary party meeting in Delhi despite the party issuing a whip. Only Arvind Sawant, Anil Desai and Rajabhau Waze attended the meeting, reaffirming their loyalty to party chief Uddhav Thackeray.

title: Why Divyanka Tripathi had only soups, salads during pregnancy: ‘Was eating chaat also’

California lawmakers pass UK and Canada-like software tax that US 'fought' to remove
description: California is set to implement a new sales tax on digital software downloads, impacting platforms like Slack and Google Workspace starting next year. Governor Newsom expects the measure, projected to generate $900 million annually, to primarily affect businesses. This move draws parallels to international digital services taxes, though California's approach expands existing sales tax to digital purchases for fairness, aiming to equalize tax burdens between physical and online software buyers.

title: No relief for woman advocate accused of creating ruckus at police station
Toronto Strengthens Defense with Blockbuster $68M Lightning Acquisition
description: John Chayka did not ease into the Toronto Maple Leafs job. His first major swing brought Darren Raddysh from Tampa Bay in a reported $68 million sign-and-trade, giving Toronto a new power-play weapon and a clear defensive reset after a rough season with pressure attached to the price and term. Chayka now owns the bet. So does Toronto’s blue line.

title: Sitharaman says external aid projects should help Northeast products reach global markets
description: Union finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman emphasized that externally aided projects in the Northeast should empower local products for global markets and strengthen livelihoods. She highlighted a seven-fold increase in assistance, with over 10,000 km of roads built since 2014. Sitharaman underscored the region's potential, stating that EAPs offer a development model combining central support, state execution, and global expertise for local communities.

Why Ronaldo's FIFA World Cup opener wasn't as simple as zero goals
description: Cristiano Ronaldo's blank outing against DR Congo reignited questions over his place in Portugal's World Cup side. But the numbers paint a more complicated picture. While the 41-year-old failed to register a shot on target, FIFA tracking data shows he made 47 off-ball runs and was found only 10 times. His finishing deserves scrutiny, but Portugal's lack of service and creativity may have been just as responsible for a frustrating opening night.

title: Ex-DRDO scientist accused of sharing secrets with Pakistani agent to face trial
description: A former DRDO scientist, Pradeep Kurulkar, faces charges in an espionage case, with trial set to begin next month. Accused of sharing classified defence information with a suspected Pakistani intelligence agent, Kurulkar's plea for discharge was rejected. Proceedings will be held in-camera due to the sensitive nature of the Official Secrets Act violations.

description: Ahead of the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination on June 21, the National Testing Agency (NTA) will conduct a nationwide mock drill on June 20. This measure aims to ensure a seamless process for the nearly 23 lakh candidates. The NTA is actively communicating with students, urging them to download fresh admit cards and warning against fraudulent messages.

title: Mob mistakes couple for child kidnappers, assaults them; 16 arrested
description: Two young development workers, on a fellowship field visit in Odisha's Rayagada district, were brutally attacked by a mob who mistook them for child kidnappers. Despite showing identity cards and explaining their purpose, the duo was chased, assaulted, and the woman was half-stripped. Police have arrested 16 individuals and are searching for more accused, registering four cases related to the terrifying ordeal.

title: Ryan Ten Doeschate names the ideal successor for Hardik Pandya
14-year-old sold for Rs 32,000 under pretext of marriage, gangraped; 4 arrested
description: Authorities in Ajmer, Rajasthan, have apprehended four individuals in connection with the trafficking and gang-rape of a 14-year-old girl. The minor was allegedly sold for Rs 32,000 under the guise of marriage. Rescued from Churu district, the girl's statement and medical evidence led to charges under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and POCSO Act. Investigations are ongoing to locate a fifth suspect.

title: The costs of the Iran war: Thousands of lives and billions of dollars
Bihar raises circle rates: Here's what it means for property buyers and sellers
description: Bihar's property registration rates have significantly increased, with rural land rates jumping 60% and urban rates doubling. An annual 5% MVR hike and a 1% stamp duty increase are now in effect. This move aims to boost farmer compensation for land acquisition, which will see substantial hikes for both state and central projects. Women buyers will also benefit from an increased stamp duty concession.

title: First US-Iran talks in Switzerland postponed as Lebanon violence threatens peace deal

description: Generative AI is poised to revolutionize India's real estate, potentially adding $14-17 billion over seven years by boosting efficiency and decision-making. This technology promises faster project launches, accelerated sales, and cost reductions. Beyond construction, AI will enhance the home-buying experience through personalized recommendations and better understanding of buyer needs, leading to more responsive housing solutions.

With mediation efforts from Qatar, the US, and Iran, this agreement seeks to ease tensions and avert a larger conflict in the region. Although neither party has commented publicly, there is cautious optimism for peace and stability in areas affected by the turmoil.

He also linked India's Test struggles to Gambhir's tenure, advocating for a mentor over a coach.

description: With a US sanctions waiver in place for three months, India stepped up Russian crude oil procurement, and import levels touched near all-time highs. India's world-class airports and the first-impression challenge facing its cities
description: Many Indian cities struggle to impress travellers from the air, often revealing informal settlements and pollution, unlike the orderly, greener landscapes of Singapore, Bangkok, or Colombo. This stark contrast stems from rapid urbanisation, immense population density, and governance challenges, despite India's economic growth. While infrastructure is improving, the sheer scale of the problem presents a significant hurdle.
